task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: Explanation and use of the PQT V2.1 test chart (Extended description)

abstract.p: This document provides an explanation on how this chart should be used.
This chart is meant to check if the print quality of the printer is correct

prereq.p: To use the PQT V2.1 test chart properly, the following requirements should be met:

li.p: All engine installation calibrations should be done.
PHD-, media- and printer calibration.
Custom media calibration must be completed for the media in use, with MPI3000 as the reference.
Drop-down mode should be used (no winder).

note.p: When printing the PQT test chart, it is necessary to select a media. Depending on the choice made, different settings will be applied to the test prints.

li.p: Service tests media Vinyl/Film/Paper:
The default media settings are used for each of the test prints. They cannot be changed.
All customer media:
The media related settings are applied as defined in the RIP.
Incorrect settings can result in PQ artifacts.
The color profile settings for this print are fixed and will not be taken from the media profile.

note.p: When judging the print quality of the PQT chart use this document for reference :

section.title: This is how the PQT V2.1 test chart looks like

entry.p: Information area containing serial number, ECID, printmode, printhead information
Green bars to detect media step defects
Single color field cyan, to analyze the quality of the colors or printhead
Double color field cyan/magenta, to analyze the quality of the colors or printheads
Single color field magenta, to analyze the quality of the colors or printhead
Double color field magenta/yellow, to analyze the quality of the colors or printheads
Single color field yellow, to analyze the quality of the colors or printhead
Double color field cyan/yellow, to analyze the quality of the colors or printheads
Black only field, to analyze the color quality, printheads and curing (gloss-banding and wrinkle)
Color wedge, can be used to determine the color gamut in combination with the used media.
These bars can be used to determine the level of smudge (how well is the upper ink layer cured).
Targets to judge the readability, the sharpness.
Color to color alignment targets (color registration). This area also contains the 1K area.
Smooth density bars, to judge visually the graininess.
Single color targets to judge printhead quality.

section.title: For all colors light lines (~1mm) or OD differences per swath (area B / I )

li.p: Per print swath a repeating light or dark lines are seen at the swath boundary.
Sometimes changes in OD per swath, light or dark area stripes around the swath boundary.
Too large random media step errors.
All swath interweaving areas lighter or darker.
In the 4-pass mode (and higher) these defects should not be seen.

section.title: Density variations per swath (OD-banding) the same pattern (P)

li.p: OD banding, is that per print swath repeating variations in density can be seen.
The more passes per print mode, the smaller the swaths become and the less visible the defect.
In the 4-pass mode (and higher) these defects should not be seen. In practice acceptable light OD variations around the swath boundary can be visible.
Gloss-banding, swath boundary and zebra-effects are different types of disturbances in the gloss of the print.
But for all the gloss is in the direction of the carriage and repeats every swath.
Gloss banding may not be seen in the 4-pass mode. In practice for acceptable swath banding can be seen using the prints in the 4-pass mode.
They can be seen as rough repeating gloss patterns within the swath, especially in black and mixtures of black and yellow.
During the pre-curing the surface of the ink layer deforms in wrinkles.
In most cases the wrinkle patterns are related to the swath width of the print mode.
Print should be free of wrinkles as for the all print modes.

section.title: Dots not ordered uniform (graininess) (area N / O / P)

li.p: Graininess is the visibility of the ink drops on the printed media.
This visibility becomes better (worse) at low coverage, when the contrast is high (e.g. black ink), drop size increases or the dot position becomes less accurate.
Area with 15% coverage should look very regular, not grainy (clustering of dots).

section.title: Light horizontal lines (streaks) (C / D / E / F / G / H)

li.p: Streaks are thin lines in the carriage direction, mostly repeating every swath.
Mostly on one color only.
Streaks should not be visible. Check also the printheads by using the printhead quality chart.
In an inked area (seen in blue and sometimes black) at random places white needle shaped holes can be seen, due to retraction/wetting issues.
In some cases locally the surface of the media is not coated, fibers can be seen.
Brittle is when the amount of white spots is that high that this leads to a drop in density.
Fish-eyes are due to local wetting issues due to dust during printing.
Retracting points may not be visible in the 8-pass mode. In practice retraction prints can be seen for some media.

section.title: Sharpness of lines/text (1K-area of N / M)

li.p: The printed text and lines should have sharp edged.
4pt positive letter type should be readable with ‘open’ characters in the 1K field. In the square colored targets the white lines should stay visible.
The colorspace of the inks is that large that it should at least cover the colorspace of FOGRA39, an important color profile for the pre-press industry.
The target is also added to measure the gamut for media at the custom side.
For the 8-pass mode the volume of the printer should be larger than FOGRA39.
During the customer media calibration procedure, the height of the carriage is tuned to the thickness of the used media.
The distance printhead nozzle plate to the media should be 1.80 mm.
4pt positive letter type should be readable with ‘open’ characters in the 1K-area of the N field, inverse text.
In the square colored targets the white lines should stay visible.
